# Butter LLM Entry Point

Butter is a compact UI design skill for AI coding agents. Use it when designing,
building, or reviewing React, Tailwind, shadcn, or frontend interfaces.

Core directive: make every touched UI smoother, more tactile, more polished,
and more animated. Butter should improve motion, visual hierarchy, spacing,
state clarity, and text/number transitions everywhere it is applied.

Butter must not make everything look the same. It chooses a shape character,
surface character, and motion signature per task. It also respects explicit
user constraints such as no animation, minimal, no rounded UI, existing brand,
or do not change layout.

If an element is unknown, Butter still builds it at high quality by identifying
the job, choosing a compact object silhouette, defining states, adding one
state carrier, and animating changing labels or values.

Selectable skill files:
- `SKILL.md` - full Butter core, load order, values, quality gate
- `visual/SKILL.md` - radius, color, type, spacing, surface rules
- `motion/SKILL.md` - springs, morphs, progress timing, text motion
- `patterns/SKILL.md` - reusable Butter UI component patterns
- `construction/SKILL.md` - implementation rules that protect design quality

Load order for real UI work:
1. Read `SKILL.md`.
2. Read `visual/SKILL.md`.
3. Read `motion/SKILL.md`.
4. Read `patterns/SKILL.md`.
5. Read `construction/SKILL.md` when writing code.

Do not use only this file for implementation. This file is an index and
orientation layer; the Butter subskills contain the actual design rules.

Download guidance:
- Want one slash command: install only `skills/SKILL.md`.
- Want focused commands: install one or more subskills.
- Want everything: install all five skill entries.

Butter quality gate:
- Improve the touched UI, not only preserve it.
- Add meaningful motion to visible state changes.
- Animate changing labels and numeric values with Butter bloom/rolling motion.
- Vary silhouette, density, accent, and motion signature so results do not look cloned.
- Respect user constraints exactly.
- Build the usable app object first, not a landing page.
- Use neutral shells with stateful accents; color is not decoration.
- Use expressive radius `24-40px` or utility radius `8-12px`.
- Keep touch targets `44-56px`.
- Use `1-2px` soft neutral borders.
- Use semibold labels, bold actions, and tabular numerals.
- Use tactile springs for object changes.
- Use linear timing for progress, recording, loading, and elapsed time.
- Expanded states must visibly morph from their collapsed trigger.
- Text must fit on mobile and desktop.

Never:
- decorative blobs
- generic gradients
- nested cards
- marketing hero composition for tools
- thin typography
- explanatory in-app copy
- spring animation for time/progress
- unrelated replacement between collapsed and expanded states
